prompt_text:
### Demographics: 62-year-old Asian woman

### Visit type: Follow-up visit (12-month follow-up)

### Reason for visit / current symptoms
Reason for visit: Routine follow-up (every 3 months) | Diagnosis context: 2 years ago Diagnosed with type 2 diabetes during urgent care evaluation for fatigue/blurred vision
Current symptoms: Reports 1-2 mild hypoglycemia episodes per month (self-resolved); no DKA/HHS signs; Nausea and reduced appetite over the last 1-2 months after starting injections.

### History
- Other conditions: Chronic kidney disease Stage 4 (15-29)
Body mass index category: Obese Class I
Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease
Rheumatoid Arthritis
Recurrent Complicated Urinary Tract Infections
Osteoporosis (on bisphosphonate therapy)
Orthopedic fracture (healed)
- Diabetes-related comorbidities: CKD: Stage 4 (15-29); HF (No); ASCVD (No); MASLD: Normal; BMI Category: Obese Class I
- Family history: No family history of type 2 diabetes (elicited)
- Social history: Former smoker (quit 1 years ago; 20 cigarettes/day for 30 years; ~30 pack-years); Alcohol 1-2 drinks/week

### Medication information
- Current medications: Glimepiride 2mg once daily; Sitagliptin 100mg once daily; Dulaglutide 1.5mg once weekly; Insulin Degludec 20units once daily; Methotrexate (15 mg weekly), Prednisone (5 mg daily), Fluticasone/salmeterol Inhaler (twice daily), Rosuvastatin (10 mg daily), Lisinopril (10 mg daily), Ciprofloxacin (500 mg twice daily for 7 days), Alendronate (70 mg weekly), Calcium Carbonate 500 Mg + Cholecalciferol 1000 Iu (daily)

### Physical exam (current)
Weight 67.7 kg; Height 153.8 cm; BMI 28.6 kg/m²; Blood pressure 115/66 mmHg

### Laboratory values (with date / relative timing)
Format: YYYY-MM-DD value or relative timing (12 months ago / 9 months ago / 6 months ago / 3 months ago / current)
Order: 12 months ago -> 9 months ago -> 6 months ago -> 3 months ago -> current
- Glycemia: HbA1c (12 months ago 9.7%; 9 months ago 10.1%; 6 months ago 10.0%; 3 months ago 10.5%; current 10.6%), FPG (12 months ago 212mg/dL; 9 months ago 289mg/dL; 6 months ago 253mg/dL; 3 months ago 298mg/dL; current 306mg/dL)
- Anthropometrics / blood pressure: Weight (12 months ago 68.9kg; 9 months ago 68.8kg; 6 months ago 67.1kg; 3 months ago 68.2kg; current 67.7kg), Height (12 months ago 153.8cm; 9 months ago 153.8cm; 6 months ago 153.8cm; 3 months ago 153.8cm; current 153.8cm), BMI (12 months ago 29.0kg/m²; 9 months ago 29.0kg/m²; 6 months ago 29.0kg/m²; 3 months ago 29.0kg/m²; current 28.6kg/m²), SBP (12 months ago 120mmHg; 9 months ago 120mmHg; 6 months ago 120mmHg; 3 months ago 120mmHg; current 115mmHg), DBP (12 months ago 66mmHg; 9 months ago 65mmHg; 6 months ago 66mmHg; 3 months ago 65mmHg; current 66mmHg)
- Liver function: ALT (12 months ago 24U/L; 9 months ago 18U/L; 6 months ago 23U/L; 3 months ago 23U/L; current 20U/L), AST (12 months ago 20U/L; 9 months ago 15U/L; 6 months ago 15U/L; 3 months ago 14U/L; current 18U/L), Total Bilirubin (12 months ago 1.2mg/dL; 9 months ago 1.2mg/dL; 6 months ago 1.2mg/dL; 3 months ago 1.2mg/dL; current 1.2mg/dL)
- Kidney: eGFR (12 months ago 27mL/min/1.73m²; 9 months ago 28mL/min/1.73m²; 6 months ago 26mL/min/1.73m²; 3 months ago 25mL/min/1.73m²; current 22mL/min/1.73m²), UACR (12 months ago 826mg/g; 9 months ago 895mg/g; 6 months ago 923mg/g; 3 months ago 711mg/g; current 963mg/g)
- Lipids: LDL (12 months ago 83mg/dL; 9 months ago 82mg/dL; 6 months ago 81mg/dL; 3 months ago 80mg/dL; current 84mg/dL); HDL (12 months ago 47mg/dL; 9 months ago 47mg/dL; 6 months ago 47mg/dL; 3 months ago 46mg/dL; current 45mg/dL); Triglycerides (12 months ago 162mg/dL; 9 months ago 180mg/dL; 6 months ago 165mg/dL; 3 months ago 183mg/dL; current 172mg/dL)
- Hemoglobin: Hemoglobin (12 months ago 11.0g/dL; 9 months ago 11.0g/dL; 6 months ago 10.2g/dL; 3 months ago 10.0g/dL; current 10.3g/dL)
- Additional tests: urine ketone Not measured (Current); fasting C-peptide Not measured (Current)

### Individualized treatment decision factors
Current adherence and self-management: Very poor adherence (estimated <50%); SMBG rarely performed; lifestyle control severely limited.
Financial context: Patient has standard insurance with moderate copays and a monthly medication budget of ≤ KRW 500,000.
